page: Hetzner Cloud cx53 is an x86_64 virtual server powered by an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 16 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with a shared CPU allocation model. The system is equipped with 31.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 1.95 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 320 GB SSD, while no dedicated GPU hardware is present.

In benchmark evaluations, the cx53 demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25% of tested instances. Multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench scores, and Passmark metrics generally fall within the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests, including both cached small-block reads and uncached large-block read/write operations, also register average performance. For specialized workloads, the server achieves strong text generation speeds during LLM inference with small 135M models, though performance scales down to average levels for medium 7B models.

Given its shared CPU allocation and hardware profile, the cx53 offers a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring high core counts without the premium of dedicated CPU threads. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it suitable for compute-heavy tasks that do not demand massive memory footprints. Recommended workloads include multi-threaded file decompression, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations.
description: Hetzner Cloud cx53 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs operating at 2.0 GHz and 31.0 GB of RAM. It includes a bundled 320 GB local SSD and operates on the QEMU hypervisor without any dedicated GPU resources. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and strong text generation speeds for small LLM models, while multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and shared CPU allocation, this server provides a cost-effective solution for multi-threaded applications. It is well-suited for software compilation, static web serving, and general-purpose workloads that benefit from high core counts but do not require dedicated CPU resources.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cx53 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 31.0 GB of RAM, and a 320 GB local SSD, delivering strong single-core performance for compute-focused workloads.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud cx53 is an x86_64 server with 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 31.0 GB RAM, and a 320 GB SSD, optimized for budget-conscious compute workloads.
tagline: A cost-efficient virtual server featuring sixteen shared AMD EPYC cores and local SSD storage for compute-focused workloads.
bullet_points: 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
31.0 GB of system memory and 320 GB of local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and small-model LLM text generation performance
Shared CPU allocation model for cost-efficient resource scaling
Suitable for software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression
